response:
Short answer: by searching the raw Service Bus operational blobs in the storage account (because ops logs were only forwarded to Datadog), we found the specific tracking id and payload that showed repeated GetSubscription attempts using an entity-level SAS that lacked Manage rights.

What we did and found (concise timeline):
- Noted repeated 40100 Unauthorized GetSubscription failures in prod-azure-v3.
- Service Bus operational logs were not in Log Analytics (forwarded to Datadog), so we searched the raw blob logs in storage account ddlogstorage3a0c161f2e70.
- Located the exact payload with TrackingId 7b845798-c613-49fa-9cc3-c49ca97cce31_G17 and the matching operational-log record (insights-logs-operationallogs, 2026-04-15 ~16:48:51 UTC).
- The record showed: EventName = "Retrieve Subscription", Status = "Failed", Caller = "ServiceBus Client", Via = sb://prod-azure-v3.servicebus.windows.net/pim-dev/Subscriptions/check-reader?api-version=2021-05&enrich=True, ErrorCode = Unauthorized, ErrorMessage = SubCode=40100 Unauthorized access for GetSubscription.
- The same hour showed ~442–450 repeated attempts per endpoint against five pim-dev subscription endpoints (check-reader, check-contributor, get-eligibility, request-reader, request-contributor).
- Checked Service Bus auth rules: only RootManageSharedAccessKey at namespace level; the topic pim-dev had an entity SAS rule named pim with Listen,Send but NO Manage.
- Confirmed the offending app connection string (app_pim-backend-dev_conn) used SharedAccessKeyName=pim and EntityPath=pim-dev (i.e., the pim SAS), which does not grant Manage and therefore cannot call GetSubscription.

Conclusion: the 40100 errors were traced to a client using the entity-level 'pim' SAS (Listen/Send only) to call subscription-management (GetSubscription) operations that require Manage, causing the Unauthorized errors.

{"ns": "azure-msp", "task": "debug", "facts": ["APIM authentication debugging pattern → targets → APIM", "APIM authentication debugging pattern → uses → APIM policy XML", "APIM authentication debugging pattern → used_by → auth debugging sessions", "APIM authentication debugging pattern → integrates_with → agent-fleet-api", "APIM authentication debugging pattern → used_by → ApiManagementGatewayLogs", "APIM authentication debugging pattern → uses → APIM on-error policy", "APIM authentication debugging pattern → implements → APIM rewrite logic (/v1 preservation)", "APIM authentication debugging pattern → configured_with → api/openapi-apim.yaml", "APIM authentication debugging pattern → integrates_with → App Insights", "APIM authentication debugging pattern → targets → Application Gateway", "APIM authentication debugging pattern → contained_in → azr-fleet-platform-infra", "APIM authentication debugging pattern → integrates_with → azr-fleet-platform-prod", "APIM authentication debugging pattern → depends_on → Canonical PolicyEngine.Read/Write/Admin roles"], "recent": ["2026-04-15: On 2026-04-15, traced Service Bus 40100 unauthorized GetSubscription errors in namespace prod-azure-v3 to a specific client pattern and likely source app. Service Bus operational logs were only configured to Datadog forwarding storage, not Log Analytics, so the raw blob logs in storage account ddlogstorage3a0c161f2e70 had to be searched. The exact payload with TrackingId 7b845798-c613-49fa-9cc3-c49ca97cce31_G17 was found in insights-logs-operationallogs for 2026-04-15 hour 16. Matching record: EventTimeString 4/15/2026 4:48:51 PM +00:00, EventName 'Retrieve Subscription', Status 'Failed', Caller 'ServiceBus Client', Via 'sb://prod-azure-v3.servicebus.windows.net/pim-dev/Subscriptions/check-reader?api-version=2021-05&enrich=True', ErrorCode Unauthorized, ErrorMessage SubCode=40100 Unauthorized access for GetSubscription. Same hour showed repeated failures from ServiceBus Client against five pim-dev subscription management endpoints: check-reader, check-contributor, get-eligibility, request-reader, request-contributor, with roughly 442-450 attempts each in that hour. Service Bus namespace auth rules showed only RootManageSharedAccessKey at namespace level. Topic pim-dev had topic-sco
[specifics: storage_account: ddlogstorage3a0c161f2e70; tracking_id: 7b845798-c613-49fa-9cc3-c49ca97cce31_G17; servicebus_namespace: prod-azure-v3; operation_error: GetSubscription=40100 Unauthorized; via_uri: sb://prod-azure-v3.servicebus.windows.net/pim-dev/Subscriptions/check-reader?api-version=2021-05&enrich=True; subscription_endpoints: check-reader, check-contributor, get-eligibility, request-reader, request-contributor; attempt_count: ~442-450 attempts per endpoint in the hour; namespace_auth: only RootManageSharedAccessKey present at namespace level; topic_sas_rule: pim (EntityPath=pim-dev) rights=Listen,Send (no Manage); app_pim-backend-dev_conn: SERVICE_BUS_CONNECTION_STRING contains Endpoint=sb://prod-azure-v3.servicebus.windows.net/; SharedAccessKeyName=pim; EntityPath=pim-dev]", "2026-04-15: On 2026-04-15, investigation of fazure-mcp 500/502/403 traffic found that the current issue was not WAF regression and not backend 500s. In AGWAccessLogs for fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com over the prior 24h, there were 0 HTTP 500 responses, multiple 502s concentrated around 13:46-13:50 UTC and 14:14-14:15 UTC and especially 14:38-14:51 UTC, and a small number of 403s. AGWFirewallLogs showed trusted Rackspace/Janus traffic matching AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp and current 403s were mainly expected VpnOnly blocks from external/scanner or preview IPs such as 146.70.189.237 and 52.123.190.89/125. The fazure-mcp backend is App Service azure-mcp-prod in rg-azure-mcp-prod with linked App Insights azure-mcp-prod. App Service state was Running and App Gateway show-backend-health reported the backend healthy. App Service metrics showed Requests and Http2xx continuing through the window and Http5xx stayed 0 throughout, confirming the backend was not returning server 500s. App Gateway metrics showed HealthyHostCount stayed 1 and UnhealthyHostCount stayed 0 throughout the incident while FailedRequests spiked, indicating short transient backend unavailability that did not trip health-state change
[specifics: app_service: azure-mcp-prod (rg-azure-mcp-prod); app_insights: azure-mcp-prod; probe_config: host=azure-mcp-prod.prodengineeringase.p.azurewebsites.net; path=/health; interval=300s; timeout=30s; unhealthyThreshold=3; other_probes_interval: 30s; backend_http_setting: fazure-mcp-backend; protocol=HTTPS; port=443; requestTimeout=20s; workers: numberOfWorkers=1; health_counts: HealthyHostCount=1; UnhealthyHostCount=0; http5xx: Http5xx=0; manual_restart: daniel.temalatzi@rackspace.com from 72.32.180.178; cert_issue: managed certificate renewal failed because DNS pointed to App Gateway IP=20.118.70.227 instead of App Service IP]", "2026-04-03: On 2026-04-03 in fleet-platform-app prod telemetry, investigated two incidents during a 60-minute deployment watch. Main app incident: between 17:33:55Z and 17:34:20Z, send_bulk_operation_callback failed with RetryableHTTPError HTTP 500 after 4 attempts from BULK_AUTOMATION_CALLBACK_URL=https://governance.mscloud.rackspace.com/policy-engine/v2/bulk-operations/callback. Multiple distinct returned correlationIds were observed (including 9e3e8b0f-43ce-4c05-8885-1c8b48dc88be and 7c189fbd-4a41-45b1-af1e-90835f0874c2). This maps to policy_rule_events.py callback send path and bulk_callback_client/http_client retry behavior, but prod-azr-compliance-v3 App Insights showed 164 BulkOperationCallback requests in the same 17:33:30Z-17:35:30Z window and all were HTTP 200, with no visible backend 500s. Conclusion: likely external governance/policy-engine/APIM layer issue in front of the callback backend, not a fleet-platform internal fault after the DB migration fix. Webhook incident: at 18:19:25Z and 18:20:16Z, POST /v1/events/resource-change calls failed; APIM client-side requests cut off at ~20s and backend app requests ended as 500 after ~44s with FunctionTimeoutAbortException. Request-scope
[specifics: BULK_AUTOMATION_CALLBACK_URL: https://governance.mscloud.rackspace.com/policy-engine/v2/bulk-operations/callback; correlationIds: 9e3e8b0f-43ce-4c05-8885-1c8b48dc88be, 7c189fbd-4a41-45b1-af1e-90835f0874c2; retry_attempts: 4; retryable_http_error: HTTP 500; prod-azr-compliance-v3 BulkOperationCallback_count: 164 (all HTTP 200); apim_client_cutoff: ~20s; backend_timeout: ~44s (FunctionTimeoutAbortException); servicebus_error: ServiceBusAuthenticationError / 'CBS Authentication Expired'; missing_trace: WebhookEnqueueFailure trace not emitted for failed operation IDs; servicebus_cleanup_blocking: _servicebus_sender_context cleanup awaits _close_sender_slot and _retire_servicebus_transport without timeout]", "2026-04-01: April 1, 2026 prod App Insights triage for fleet platform: main app azr-fleet-platform-prod showed no active exceptions or failed requests in the queried 24h window for resource_change_ingest_sb_trigger after prior fixes. Remaining active failures were in azr-fleet-webhook-prod: 4 recent WebhookEnqueueFailure 503s caused by transient Microsoft.ServiceBus send timeouts during sender.send_messages. Patched functions-webhook/function_app.py to retry once on transient send timeout using a fresh retired transport/client while keeping the same overall operation timeout budget, and added regression tests in tests/test_webhook_token_validation.py. Also added the missing _SERVICEBUS_CLIENT_LOCK global declaration.
[specifics: app_service: azr-fleet-platform-prod; function_trigger: resource_change_ingest_sb_trigger; app_service: azr-fleet-webhook-prod; webhook_enqueue_failures=4 (HTTP 503); error_location: Microsoft.ServiceBus send timeouts during sender.send_messages; patched_file: functions-webhook/function_app.py; added_test: tests/test_webhook_token_validation.py; global_decl_added: _SERVICEBUS_CLIENT_LOCK; retry_behavior: retry once on transient ServiceBus send timeout using a fresh retired transport/client while preserving overall operation timeout budget]", "2026-03-20: On 2026-03-20, live Azure investigation of Log Analytics workspace DefaultWorkspace-5ad08c08-96bb-45c0-a567-8bcc86020661-SCUS in subscription 5ad08c08-96bb-45c0-a567-8bcc86020661 (Azure Engineering - Production Subscription) found AppExceptions billed usage of 1552.998 GB over 90 days. 95.5% of this was from App Insights component prod-azure-audit-engine / Function App prod-azure-audit-engine in resource group ussc-prod-audit-engine-rg001. Dominant signature: Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus.ServiceBusException at AmqpConnectionScope.CreateReceivingLinkAsync with 101,940,682 rows and 1483.83 GB from 2026-01-28 onward at ~25-34 GB/day. Sample messages showed MessagingEntityNotFound 404s against missing Service Bus entities in namespace fazure-janus-sb-primary, especially partner-center-agreements/Subscriptions/pc-agreements-sub (~646.37 GB), arc-lh-deployment/Subscriptions/arclhdeploymentsub (~645.78 GB), decommissioning-events/Subscriptions/decom-process (~95.02 GB), and decommissioning-events/Subscriptions/fetch-ri-instances (~94.95 GB). Verified topics partner-center-agreements and arc-lh-deployment do not exist. Verified function app settings still reference these names: agreementDate
[specifics: log_analytics_workspace: DefaultWorkspace-5ad08c08-96bb-45c0-a567-8bcc86020661-SCUS (subscription 5ad08c08-96bb-45c0-a567-8bcc86020661); app_exceptions_billed_usage: 1552.998 GB (90 days); dominant_component: prod-azure-audit-engine Function App in resource group ussc-prod-audit-engine-rg001 contributed 95.5% (~1483.83 GB); error_signature: Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus.ServiceBusException at AmqpConnectionScope.CreateReceivingLinkAsync; rows=101,940,682; size=1483.83 GB; rate≈25-34 GB/day; servicebus_namespace: fazure-janus-sb-primary; servicebus_subscription_sizes: pc-agreements-sub=646.37 GB, arclhdeploymentsub=645.78 GB, decom-process=95.02 GB, fetch-ri-instances=94.95 GB; missing_topics: partner-center-agreements, arc-lh-deployment (verified nonexistent); activity_log: host restart/host sync by vikesh.agarwal@rackspace.com]", "2026-03-20: On 2026-03-20 during prod health validation of fleet-platform-app after PR #59 deployment, azr-fleet-platform-prod main app was healthy but azr-fleet-webhook-prod was in an active outage. Public and direct webhook health endpoints returned 500, while direct main /api/health returned 200. App Insights over the prior 30 minutes showed about 71,770 webhook requests with about 70,524 failures. Dominant exceptions were System.InvalidOperationException 'Did not find any initialized language workers' and FunctionTimeoutException at 00:00:30 for Functions.eventgrid_resource_change_webhook. 5-minute bins showed webhook p95 around 32s from 19:10-19:35 UTC. Service Bus namespace azr-fleet-platform-events showed no ServerErrors/UserErrors/ThrottledRequests, and ServerSendLatency averaged roughly 307-763 ms, so namespace-side failure was not the primary cause. Root-cause inference: current webhook code opens and closes a Service Bus sender per request and only wraps sender.send_messages() in wait_for; sender acquisition/teardown is outside the timeout guard, allowing requests to sit until the Functions host kills workers. A local hotfix was prepared to use a reusable sender pool per worker proc
[specifics: app_service: azr-fleet-webhook-prod=active outage; health_endpoint_status: webhook endpoints=500; main /api/health=200; app_insights_webhook_requests: 71770 total, 70524 failures (30m); dominant_exception: System.InvalidOperationException='Did not find any initialized language workers'; function_timeout: Functions.eventgrid_resource_change_webhook=00:00:30; webhook_p95_latency: ~32s (5-min bins); servicebus_namespace: azr-fleet-platform-events=no ServerErrors/UserErrors/ThrottledRequests; servicebus_server_send_latency: ~307-763 ms average; root_cause: sender opened/closed per request; sender acquisition/teardown outside wait_for leading to worker kill; hotfix: reusable Service Bus sender pool per worker; bind acquire+send under timeout]"]}
